claim: MCP is fundamentally a mega context problem: tools themselves take up large token budgets when enumerated (Cloudflare's 2600 endpoints → 1.1M tokens in tool definitions), making progressive discovery essential.
actionable: Don't enumerate all available tools in context; instead implement progressive discovery (CLI-style, tool search, or code generation) to load only relevant tools on demand.

claim: Tool search (like in Claude Code) loads K most-relevant tools into context via keyword matching, but wastes tokens on unused tools; code generation via typed SDKs is more efficient.
actionable: For large APIs, prefer generating typed SDKs and letting the model write code against types rather than describing tools as JSON in context.

claim: CLI-style introspection works well for agents (discovering commands via --help, progressive drill-down) but requires shell access, which isn't always available.
actionable: If you can provide shell access, CLIs are a self-discoverable, naturally-documented interface for agents; otherwise fall back to code generation or tool search.

claim: Running untrusted code generated by LLMs in isolated workers (like Cloudflare Workers) with programmable guardrails (network access control, filesystem sandboxing) is safe and more expressive than tool calling.
actionable: For agents needing broad API access, generate code and run it in a sandbox (Workers, Deno, Pydantic Monty) with programmable guardrails rather than predefined tool calls.

claim: Types are a concise way to represent API inputs/outputs (smaller token budget than JSON tool definitions); agents can reason about types and generate code more effectively.
actionable: When exposing APIs to agents, generate TypeScript types from OpenAPI specs and let agents write code against types instead of describing every endpoint.

claim: Future MCP client pattern: stateless agent loops with programmatic tool calling, agents saving generated code as mini-scripts for re-use and cron scheduling.
actionable: Design agent systems to generate reusable code scripts that can be scheduled, version-controlled, and re-run without regenerating code each time.

claim: MCP servers will become native middleware in web frameworks; the SDK is shrinking to express only the protocol itself, making it silly not to have MCP in every framework.
actionable: Expect MCP to become a native flag in Node/Python frameworks by end of 2025; default to enabling MCP=true on your APIs.

claim: Cloudflare demonstrated giving agents access to 2600+ API endpoints via code generation + sandboxed execution, proving complete API surface accessibility is possible and practical.
actionable: For APIs with hundreds of endpoints, implement code generation + sandboxed execution rather than splitting into multiple MCP servers.

claim: Rate limiting on APIs is critical when agents can call endpoints in loops; APIs need defenses against agent hammer attacks and resource exhaustion.
actionable: Harden your API rate limiting and quotas; agents will call endpoints far more aggressively than humans and can amplify abuse if unchecked.
